title: Bubblefest
description: Things are popping at Discovery Cube OC as crowd-favorite Bubblefest returns for another Spring Break. A highlight is the Mega Bubblefest Laser Show, starring renowned bubble artist Melody Yang. This fun and exciting stage show combines music, lasers, and bubble artistry.

title: Nowruz Festival: The Vines of Love
description: The Central Library celebrates Nowruz, the Persian New Year, and the respective cultural heritage of the Persian community of Los Angeles. The day features performances, author events in both English and Farsi, a Persian book fair, games and crafts, workshops, and more. Highlights:.

title: Marine Mammal Care Center Family Science Sunday
description: Families can visit the Marine Mammal Care Center on the 3rd Sunday of every month for the hands-on Family Science Sunday program. This one-hour session is led by MMCC experts, who dive into topics such as ocean conservation, marine mammals, and beyond.

title: A Chance to Dance by the Los Angeles Ballet Company
description: Los Angeles Ballet is on my mind, because I just went to an evening program celebrating its 20th year! So I wanted to highlight that on select Sundays, LAB dancers offer free ballet classes and more. (For kids and adults!).

title: Mummies of the World at the California Science Center
description: It's back! I saw this on its first tour here in 2010, and it was amazing, and now it's back with a new selection of specimens never-before-seen in Los Angeles. The exhibit unravels the science of mummification, bringing together over 30 naturally and intentionally preserved human and animal mummies from South America, Europe, and ancient Egypt.

title: Studio Ghibli's Ponyo Exhibition
description: Ponyo tells the tale of a magical goldfish and her friendship with a human child. It's also the favorite Ghibli film of many of the kids I know. The new exhibition includes over 100 materials including art boards, posters, a Studio Ghibli animation desk, and original hand drawings by Hayao Miyazaki. There are screenings and family workshops, too.

title: Disney California Adventure Food & Wine Festival
description: This annual festival at Disney California Adventure is so good you can taste it! Disney California Adventure Food & Wine Festival allows guests to explore the culturally rich mix of flavors in California. New menu items for 2026 include chicharrón nachos, ramen mac & cheese, and ube latte with matcha foam.
price: Included with admission: food items available for purchase; some experiences require additional fee

title: Eggstravaganza 2026 at Disneyland Resort
description: Egg hunt- but make it Disney. Disneyland Resort hosts an epic egg hunt that requires a map. Grab your guide at a vendor location throughout the park (or in Downtown Disney) and search for hidden eggs with Disney character's faces on them, Place the stickers that came with the map on the correct locations based on what you find- when you complete the map, take it to a vendor and claim your prize. This interactive Easter activity is great for frequent parkgoers who are looking for something unique and celebratory to do at Disney.

title: Easter Eggstravaganza (Irvine Park Railroad)
description: The bunny trail leads to Irvine Park Railroad, where kids can meet the Easter Bunny, snag photo ops with him, participate in egg hunts, ride the train, and enjoy other ticketed activities.
price: Tickets for activities: $8; Book of 15 tickets: $100

title: Butterfly Season at Kidspace
description: The Western monarch butterflies are returning to Southern California, and that’s cause for celebration at Kidspace! Butterfly Season highlights include bug safaris around the Kidspace campus, create-a-creature art projects, butterfly-themed fantasy dress-up and play, and "flower showers" to help flowers grow. This year, there is a new interpretation of artist Christopher Lutter’s handmade Monarch butterflies, crafted from upcycled waste-stream materials.
price: General admission applies, (caterpillar adoption starts at $8)
address: Kidspace Children's Museum, 480 N. Arroyo Blvd, 91103

title: Open House at Tanaka Farms
description: Everyone is welcome down on the farm! Tanaka Farms celebrates its annual Open House. Admission is free, and guests are treated to complimentary tractor rides and giveaways. Other activities during Open House weekend include games and activities, photos with the Easter Bunny, local vendors and information booths, and visits with the barnyard animals. Due to expected crowds, parking passes are required for this activity ($6 per car).

title: Queen Mary St. Patrick's Day Celebration
description: The Queen Mary welcomes families for a high-spirited St. Patrick’s Day weekend celebration packed with festive cheer. Guests can enjoy green drinks and delicious bites while dancing along to Irish tunes spun by a live DJ. Plus traditional Celtic dancers are aboard for special performances.

title: The Oscars  (Academy Awards)
description: "And the winner is." The 98th Oscars ceremony airs live on ABC and Hulu. This year's event is held at Hollywood's Dolby Theatre, with returning host Conan O'Brien.
price: Free to watch on streaming or broadcast

title: Smorgasburg Los Angeles
description: Smorgasburg LA is a weekly market for food, design, vintage, and events, open every Sunday at ROW DTLA in Downtown Los Angeles.

title: Game On!  at the California Science Center
description: Get a move on - the latest exhibition at the California Science Center looks at the intersection of science, sports, and play! Game On! celebrates the transformative power of play and the joy of the human body in motion.

title: Sunmist Fruit Farm
description: Sunmist Fruit Farm specializes in cultivating and harvesting premium tangerines and lemons, the farm is committed to providing fresh and high-quality produce to the local community.
price: Entry Fee: $5 per person; children 2 years and under are free

title: Jaws: The Exhibition
description: Just when you thought it was safe to go back in the museum. The newest temporary exhibition of the Academy Museum of Motion Pictures looks at Steven Spielberg’s groundbreaking blockbuster film Jaws (1975).
city: The Academy Museum of Motion Pictures
locationName: The Academy Museum of Motion Pictures
price: Included with admission; (Visitors 17 and under get in free)

title: Tidepool Walks
description: Cabrillo Marine Aquarium educators conduct tours of the Point Fermin tide pools. Venture out on a naturalist-led ramble on the rocks to see animals in their natural habitat.

description: Hollywood isn’t a place.It’s a puppet show! The people and places of the City of Angels come to puppet life in Hooray LA! at The Bob Baker Marionette Theater.

title: St. Joseph's Day & The Return of the Swallows
description: St. Joseph's Day marks the annual return of the swallows to San Juan Capistrano. The mission's celebration is marked by pageantry, the traditional bell ringing at the sight of the first swallows, and a celebration with musical performances, tours, demonstrations, and food vendors.
price: Adults $20; Seniors: $17; Students & Children (5+) $14; Children 4 and under are free

title: Locals’ Night at Santa Monica Pier
description: Santa Monica Pier is known as a tourist destination that attract guests from around the country and the world. But it's also a great place for locals to hang out! In January, I've got chills, they're multiplying: drive on in for the first ever the first-ever drive-in movie on the Santa Monica Pier: Grease!

price: Adults $12; Children/Seniors $10; Children 2 and under are free

title: Swallows Day Parade 2026
description: We all know that the swallows always return to San Juan Capistrano, but did you know that they are welcomed with an annual homecoming parade? The annual event holds the distinction of being the nation's largest non-motorized parade. It marches directly through the downtown area and is accompanied by an all-day mercado with a kids' play zone, entertainment, and more than 100 booths.

title: Gilchrist Farm Egg Hunt and Spring Fling
description: Spring has come to Gilchrist Farm. Guests can enjoy baby animals such as goats, chicks, and piglets; create spring farm crafts; and take a picture with the Easter Bunny or a friendly real bunny. Egg hunts are offered for kids of various age ranges. The most unique egg hunt here is the "challenge" course for bigger kids- where they have to work to get their loot.
price: Egg hunt: $5; Activities range from $5-$35, wristband $65

title: Underwood Farms Springtime Easter Festival 2026
description: The annual Eggs-travaganza at Underwood Family Farms returns with a whole farmful of family fun. Animal shows, tractor-drawn wagon rides, some games, and photo ops are among the activities included with admission.

title: Barnyard Egg Hunt at Mason Rec Center
description: The barnyard-themed spring event at Mason Recreation Center includes egg hunts for ages 1 through 12, moonbounces, music, arts & crafts, and games. Egg hunts are divided by age; adults can help little ones ages 1 to 2, all other kids must participate in the egg hunts on their own.

title: Venice Love Fest 2026
description: It makes a lot of sense: the festival that celebrates community and connection in Venice finally moves to Venice itself. Starting this year, the annual Venice Love Fest is being held under the Venice sign (at Windward and Pacific Aves). This free community event features live music, art and wellness, with food trucks, locally-made brands, beer gardens, and games. There's a Kids & Family Stage with all-ages programming, and fitness and wellness classes to launch the day.

title: Public Run Day Southern California Live Steamers
description: Happening in Southern California Live Steamers Station: The Southern California Live Steamers offer free rides on their miniature railroad in Wilson Park.

title: Sunmist Fruit Farm
description: Sunmist Fruit Farm specializes in cultivating and harvesting premium tangerines and lemons, the farm is committed to providing fresh and high-quality produce to the local community.
price: Entry Fee: $5 per person; children 2 years and under are free

title: Tidepool Walks
description: Cabrillo Marine Aquarium educators conduct tours of the Point Fermin tide pools. Venture out on a naturalist-led ramble on the rocks to see animals in their natural habitat.

title: Lowes Build and Grow
description: Lowe's hosts monthly workshops for children. Kids participating in the store can use tools to assemble their craft. Each participant receives a free apron, goggles, a project-themed patch, and a certification of merit upon completion.

title: Sensory Sensitive Saturdays (Cabrillo Marine Aquarium)
description: Guests can spend some quiet time enjoying the Cabrillo Marine Aquarium on Sensory Sensitive Saturdays, the third Saturday of each month, from 10am to 12pm.

description: Hollywood isn’t a place.It’s a puppet show! The people and places of the City of Angels come to puppet life in Hooray LA! at The Bob Baker Marionette Theater.

title: Bubblefest
description: Things are popping at Discovery Cube OC as crowd-favorite Bubblefest returns for another Spring Break. A highlight is the Mega Bubblefest Laser Show, starring renowned bubble artist Melody Yang. This fun and exciting stage show combines music, lasers, and bubble artistry.

title: America's Teaching Zoo Spring Spectacular 2026
description: Talk about spectacular! America's Teaching Zoo is back with its annual springtime extravaganza. The highlight of the event is always the fun presentation starring the Zoo's students and their animal friends. This year there are two brand-new scripted shows: Operation Conservation and Wild Wild West.
price: Adults $12; Children/Seniors $10; Children 2 and under are free

title: Bluey's Best Day Ever
description: Discover Bluey's Best Day Ever in Disneyland. A great family activity the whole family will love.

title: Birdwatching for Young  Children and Parents
description: Head to Malibu Lagoon State Beach for Birdwatching for Young  Children and Parents. This family event is perfect for families looking for a fun outing.

title: Mummies of the World at the California Science Center
description: It's back! I saw this on its first tour here in 2010, and it was amazing, and now it's back with a new selection of specimens never-before-seen in Los Angeles. The exhibit unravels the science of mummification, bringing together over 30 naturally and intentionally preserved human and animal mummies from South America, Europe, and ancient Egypt.

title: Studio Ghibli's Ponyo Exhibition
description: Ponyo tells the tale of a magical goldfish and her friendship with a human child. It's also the favorite Ghibli film of many of the kids I know. The new exhibition includes over 100 materials including art boards, posters, a Studio Ghibli animation desk, and original hand drawings by Hayao Miyazaki. There are screenings and family workshops, too.

title: Hoppers at the El Capitan
description: Happening in El Capitan Theatre: Human. Nature. Disney. Pixar. The new animated feature Hoppers debuts at the El Capitan.

description: This annual festival at Disney California Adventure is so good you can taste it! Disney California Adventure Food & Wine Festival allows guests to explore the culturally rich mix of flavors in California. New menu items for 2026 include chicharrón nachos, ramen mac & cheese, and ube latte with matcha foam.
price: Included with admission: food items available for purchase; some experiences require additional fee

title: Eggstravaganza 2026 at Disneyland Resort
description: Egg hunt- but make it Disney. Disneyland Resort hosts an epic egg hunt that requires a map. Grab your guide at a vendor location throughout the park (or in Downtown Disney) and search for hidden eggs with Disney character's faces on them, Place the stickers that came with the map on the correct locations based on what you find- when you complete the map, take it to a vendor and claim your prize. This interactive Easter activity is great for frequent parkgoers who are looking for something unique and celebratory to do at Disney.

title: Eggstravaganza 2026 at Disneyland Resort
description: Egg hunt- but make it Disney. Disneyland Resort hosts an epic egg hunt that requires a map. Grab your guide at a vendor location throughout the park (or in Downtown Disney) and search for hidden eggs with Disney character's faces on them, Place the stickers that came with the map on the correct locations based on what you find- when you complete the map, take it to a vendor and claim your prize. This interactive Easter activity is great for frequent parkgoers who are looking for something unique and celebratory to do at Disney.

title: Easter Eggstravaganza (Irvine Park Railroad)
description: The bunny trail leads to Irvine Park Railroad, where kids can meet the Easter Bunny, snag photo ops with him, participate in egg hunts, ride the train, and enjoy other ticketed activities.
price: Tickets for activities: $8; Book of 15 tickets: $100

title: Butterfly Season at Kidspace
description: The Western monarch butterflies are returning to Southern California, and that’s cause for celebration at Kidspace! Butterfly Season highlights include bug safaris around the Kidspace campus, create-a-creature art projects, butterfly-themed fantasy dress-up and play, and "flower showers" to help flowers grow. This year, there is a new interpretation of artist Christopher Lutter’s handmade Monarch butterflies, crafted from upcycled waste-stream materials.
price: General admission applies, (caterpillar adoption starts at $8)
address: Kidspace Children's Museum, 480 N. Arroyo Blvd, 91103

title: LA County Parks Spring Jubilee

title: Night Hike at Oak Canyon Nature Center in Anaheim (Registration Required! $7)
description: Looking for family fun in Anaheim? Check out Night Hike at Oak Canyon Nature Center in Anaheim (Registration Required! $7), a family event the kids will enjoy.

title: The Little Mermaid The Musical at the Players
description: Dive into an original musical adaptation of The Little Mermaid — based on Hans Christian Andersen’s famous fairytale. Ticket includes a free family workshop one hour prior to the Saturday 3pm and Sunday 11am matinee (no workshop before Saturday 7pm and Sunday 2pm shows).

title: Broadway Magic Hour at the Broadway Comedy Club
description: The Broadway Magic Hour features master magicians Jim Vines and Carl Mercurio in a performance that includes mind-boggling mysteries and hilarious comedy for all ages. Witness magic, mind-reading, comedy, and grand illusions! Purchase tickets online in advance. And use Code MAGIC to save 40% on tickets. Check the website for specific dates and times.

title: No Excuses No Limits  at the New Victory Theater
description: The b-boys of ILL-Abilities are flying in from four continents to bust expectations with moves so sick your head will spin. After wowing the world at the Olympics and Paralympics in Paris, nothing can stop this international all-star crew.

title: Iconic Women's Week at Children's Museum of Manhattan
description: In honor of Women’s History Month, celebrate the creativity, courage, and imagination of women who help the world grow.

title: <SOLD OUT>  St. Patrick’s Day at the Tenement Museum
description: To mark the St. Patrick’s Day weekend, families meet a costumed interpreter playing the role of Bridget Moore, an Irish immigrant. Ask her questions and learn about her life in 1860s New York. Participants also enjoy a delicious sweet treat and make a craft to celebrate.
price: $15 for adults; $7.50 for members; $10 for children

title: Brooklyn SeltzerFest and National Egg Cream Invitational in Industry City
description: The Brooklyn Seltzer Museum's lively festival features vendors, interactive experiences, live music, celebrity presenters, and thrilling competitions. Explore the rich traditions, science, and future of this beloved fizzy beverage. The museum itself is housed within the oldest seltzer works in New York City—a fourth-generation, family-run business keeping the craft of seltzer-making alive.

title: Brooklyn St. Patrick's Day Parade
description: Head to South Slope for this festive St. Patrick's Day Parade, which is the oldest St. Paddy's Day parade in Brooklyn. Music, marching bands, face painting, Irish dancers, and antique cars are all on display.
address: Starting at 15th Street & Prospect Park West

title: Aladdin & the Wonderful Lamp
description: Puppetworks has adapted the classic tale for its marionette stage. The performance is accompanied by the exotic music of Aram Khachaturian's "Gayane" ballet suite, and features spectacular effects such as a flying carpet, a vanishing castle and an ethereal Djinni of the Lamp.

title: New York International Children's Film Festival
description: NYICFF celebrates the beauty and power of film, sparks the inherent capacity of children to connect with complex, nuanced art, and encourages the creation of intelligent films that represent and celebrate unique, diverse, and historically excluded voices.

title: Classical Kids Fair at the Brooklyn Children's Museum
description: The Classical Kids Fair invites families to enjoy live performances, an instrument petting zoo, arts and crafts, radio broadcasting workshops, and more. Listen and play music along with WQXR, the Brooklyn Conservatory of Music, and more.

title: The Orchid Show 2026: Mr. Flower Fantastic's Concrete Jungle at NYBG
description: Now in its 23rd year, the Orchid Show highlights thousands of dramatically displayed orchids in the Enid A. Haupt Conservatory. In this year's show, orchids collide with concrete in a dazzling reimagining of the Big Apple from stoops and slice shops to the subway itself.

title: Hold On To Your Butts
description: It was 65 million years ago when dinosaurs ruled the Earth. Then, 30 years ago, a movie about dinosaurs ruled the box office. Now, two actors and a Foley artist perform a live, shot-for-shot parody of the greatest dinosaur film of all time. This zany, fast-paced, deliriously funny "remake" of Jurassic Park is a must see for fans of the film. Watch the movie again, then go see the show.

title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Interactive Show at Manhattan Movement and Arts
description: Four Eric Carle stories come to life through 45 magical, handcrafted puppets, world-class puppeteering and lively original music. The program includes The Very Hungry Caterpillar, From Head to Toe, The Very Quiet Cricket, and The Very Busy Spider.

title: Family Improv at Gallery Players Theatre
description: Gather the family and an afternoon of hilarious improv comedy where you help shape the show at the Gallery Players Theatre. Talented performers create scenes, characters, and songs based on your suggestions, making each show unique and unforgettable.

title: Family Art Project: Desert Dwellers at Wave Hill
description: Inspired by Wave Hill's Cactus and Succulent House, explore the plants that grow in warmer places and create prints of these resilient cacti.

title: Soccer Shots Free Fun Day
description: Soccer Shots NYC Central is hosting a Free Fun Day for children ages 2–8. Kids learn basic soccer skills, score goals, and participate in exciting games led by professional coaches. Free Fun Day Locations – Asser LevyCentral Park Brooklyn Bridge Park – Pier 5Cadman Plaza ParkJJ Byrne PlaygroundStroud PlaygroundProspect Park Check website for times/ages.
city: Various locations in Brooklyn and Manhattan
locationName: Various locations in Brooklyn and Manhattan

title: Throggs Neck St. Patrick’s Day Parade
description: The Throggs Neck Benevolent Association's annual St. Patrick's Day Parade turns out the crowds to celebrate the holiday. The parade route heads down East Tremont, toward Harding Avenue, and turns right onto Harding Avenue. There is a reviewing stand at Harding and Tremont.

title: Ok to Delay: A Case for Delaying Smart Phones and Social Media for Kids
description: This practical talk from grassroots organization, Ok To Delay, explores how smartphones and social media are reshaping childhood and how families can navigate this together. Speaker: Pedro Avila, Ok To Delay Community Organizer | AI Safety Advocate and Data Science Lecturer at Columbia University. Discussion highlights: How childhood has changed Growing pressures on kids Alternatives & strategies to support kids’ wellbeing.
city: Unitarian Universalist Congregation at Shelter Rock
locationName: Unitarian Universalist Congregation at Shelter Rock

title: Hula Hoopin Queen on Theatre Row
description: In this musical from NYC Children's Theater, 10-year-old Kameeka is determined to become the Hula Hoopin’ Queen of 139th Street and finally beat her rival, Jamara Johnson. But when her mom reminds her it’s Miz Adeline’s birthday, Kameeka has to help get the party ready instead.

title: Checkmate at Brookfield Place
description: Checkmate at BFPL brings Chess to life in the Financial District, with open play, a Saturday chess skills workshop open to all ages and levels with Chess Max Academy, and more. On Thursday, March 26, World Chess Champion Grandmaster Maxim Dlugy takes on challengers in two live simulation matches, special exhibitions where an expert plays multiple opponents at once, moving from chess board to chess board as the matches unfold. Throughout the Checkmate activation, visitors can play on a larger-than-life indoor chess set.

title: Lucky Me! Celebrating St. Patrick’s Day at Children's Museum of Manhattan
description: Celebrate St. Patrick’s Day by making art inspired by symbols of luck, gratitude, and the joy of finding magic in everyday moments. Tape and Take Map Tuesday, March 17 | 2:45 pm – 3:45 pm | Ages 4 & younger | 3rd Floor | PlayWorksCelebrate St. Patrick’s Day by making your own treasure map. Tear and stick strips of washi tape to a piece of paper. Will the taped paths lead to a pot of gold? Don’t forget to add decorations using drawing tools and collage materials.

title: 14th Annual St. Patrick’s Citywide Book Day
description: The Irish Arts Center undertakes the 12th edition of one of its most beloved programs: Book Day, in which the center pays homage to Ireland’s rich literary tradition by giving away FREE books by some of the country’s—and Irish America’s—most celebrated writers.
city: Irish Arts Center (but this event is citywide)
locationName: Irish Arts Center (but this event is citywide)

title: New York City St. Patrick's Day Parade on Fifth Avenue
description: NYC turns green once again as its famous St. Patrick's Day Parade marches up Fifth Avenue, starting at 44th Street, past St. Patrick's Cathedral at 50th Street, all the way up to 79th Street and the Irish Historical Society, where the parade finishes at about 4:30-5pm.
address: Fifth Avenue at 44th Street, to 79th Street

title: The Orchid Show 2026: Mr. Flower Fantastic's Concrete Jungle at NYBG
description: Now in its 23rd year, the Orchid Show highlights thousands of dramatically displayed orchids in the Enid A. Haupt Conservatory. In this year's show, orchids collide with concrete in a dazzling reimagining of the Big Apple from stoops and slice shops to the subway itself.

title: Checkmate at Brookfield Place
description: Checkmate at BFPL brings Chess to life in the Financial District, with open play, a Saturday chess skills workshop open to all ages and levels with Chess Max Academy, and more. On Thursday, March 26, World Chess Champion Grandmaster Maxim Dlugy takes on challengers in two live simulation matches, special exhibitions where an expert plays multiple opponents at once, moving from chess board to chess board as the matches unfold. Throughout the Checkmate activation, visitors can play on a larger-than-life indoor chess set.

title: Eid al-Fitr at Children's Museum of Manhattan
description: Eid al-Fitr is a holiday celebrated by Muslims around the world marking the end of Ramadan, a month of reflection, care for others, and community. Performance & Workshops: Eid al-Fitr Celebration with the Culture Tree Friday, March 20 | 2:15 pm – 4:15 pm | All ages | 1st & 3rd FloorsEid Mubarak! Participate in an exciting afternoon with the Culture Tree.

title: Kindie Rock in Battery Park City
description: Shake all those wiggles out at Battery Park City's weekly kid concerts. Attendance is first-come, first-served, so arrive early for one of the two sessions.

description: Puppetworks has adapted the classic tale for its marionette stage. The performance is accompanied by the exotic music of Aram Khachaturian's "Gayane" ballet suite, and features spectacular effects such as a flying carpet, a vanishing castle and an ethereal Djinni of the Lamp.

title: Broadway Magic Hour at the Broadway Comedy Club
description: The Broadway Magic Hour features master magicians Jim Vines and Carl Mercurio in a performance that includes mind-boggling mysteries and hilarious comedy for all ages. Witness magic, mind-reading, comedy, and grand illusions! Purchase tickets online in advance. And use Code MAGIC to save 40% on tickets. Check the website for specific dates and times.

title: Easter Fun Cruise with Egg Hunt
description: Kids can hunt for Easter eggs while enjoying incredible views of the New York skyline on this Easter cruise. Steam under the Brooklyn Bridge, past lower Manhattan and out into the open waters of New York Harbor while the kids enjoy decorating Easter baskets, making balloon animals, getting their faces painted—and, of course, searching for Easter eggs. Mar 21, Mar 28, Mar 29 at 1pmApr 3–4 at 1pm & 3:30pm Apr 5 at 10:30am, 1pm & 3:30pm.
price: Adults $75 and up; Children $65 and up

title: Lenfest Kids: Cats and Dogs
description: Paws.and curl up with a season of family films featuring cats and dogs. Stick around after the film for free family fun with student colleagues of Columbia Artist/Teachers. Saturday, February 14, 12pm:Stuart Little (PG) Saturday, February 14, 12pm:Lady and the Tramp (PG - Live action 2019) Saturday, March 21, 12pm:The Cat Returns (G) Saturday, April 18, 12pm:Cats (PG) Saturday, April 18, 4pm:Fantastic Voyage (PG) Saturday, May 9, 12pm:Flow (PG).

title: Pysanka (The Ukrainian Easter Egg) at the Ukrainian Museum
description: Teens learn the ancient art of making pysanky - Ukrainian Easter eggs, using dyes, beeswax and a stylus to decorate eggs with traditional Ukrainian designs.

title: Art Sundae: Kelly Wall at Rockefeller Center
description: Artist Kelly Wall and Art Production Fund (APF) host a free, public, all-ages interactive art-making workshop inspired by Wall's glass sculpture practice. Kids are invited to collage lighting gel in shapes of clouds on to plexiglass magazine covers, clear mugs and key chains. The children’s artworks will then be displayed at Rockefeller Center as part of a public art exhibition.Sessions take place 10am–11:30am and 11:30am–1pm, and participants can come and go throughout their session. Register online in advance.

title: Women in Architecture at the Skyscraper Museum
description: Learn about the importance of diversity and how women architects like Zaha Hadid, Denise Scott Brown, Natalie de Blois, Maya Lin, and Jeanne Gang have designed skyscrapers and other buildings that changed city skylines. Choose your favorite featured architect and make your own creation based on their distinctive style.

title: Farmhouse Family Day: Fantastic Fibers at the Wyckoff
description: Baskets, carpets, tapestries, and clothes. These fantastic fibers created by the hands of women weave the fabric of history. Explore different techniques, styles and symbols found in fiber works around the world and try your hand at a craft of your own!

title: Spring Equinox Celebration at Concrete Plant Park
description: The Bronx River Foodway Team hosts a Spring Equinox Celebration! Welcome the start of spring with some fun activities for the family including Foodway Tour led by Journei Bimwala, herbal exploration and teas tastings with herbs grown at the Foodway in 2025, arts and crafts and more! Register online in advance. Note: Depending on weather, programming may shift to Starlight Park.

title: Hands On History: Poet-trees at King Manor Museum
description: Celebrate International Day of Forests and World Poetry Day with King Manor and Melting Metropolis! Create your own "poet-tree" with storyteller Roxanne Scott, and find a poem in the trees at Rufus King Park.

title: The Beauty of Ballet at Aaron Davis Hall
description: Have you ever wondered how ballerinas dance on their toes? Or how dancers turn without getting dizzy? Learn the answers to those questions and more during this great for families performance featuring excerpts from famous ballets including The Sleeping Beauty, Swan Lake and The Nutcracker.

title: Aladdin & the Wonderful Lamp
description: Puppetworks has adapted the classic tale for its marionette stage. The performance is accompanied by the exotic music of Aram Khachaturian's "Gayane" ballet suite, and features spectacular effects such as a flying carpet, a vanishing castle and an ethereal Djinni of the Lamp.

title: Holi Celebration at the Queens County Farm Museum
description: Welcome the arrival of spring and the harvest season with an educational program to learn about the rich cultural and spiritual significance of Holi. The program includes traditional dancers, Indian snacks and treats, and color play, where vibrant powders create a kaleidoscope of joy as participants run across the scenic south side of the 47-acre farm.

description: Whiskers, the Easter Bunny, along with all of his friends at the farm, invites families to stop by this community celebration for lots of Easter fun, including egg hunts, farm animal visits, selfie stations, a super slide, tractors to play on, picnic tables, and more.

title: Living History: The Origins of K-pop Photocards at New York Historical
description: Did you know that K-pop photocards have a connection with historical figures such as Queen Victoria and Frederick Douglass? Celebrate the return of BTS with a look at the long history of photocards. Get your own CDV made and go home with a K-pop themed freebie.

title: World Water Day at Brooklyn Museum
description: Take a deep dive into Oliver Jeffers:​ Life at Sea! at drop-in workshops celebrating colorful creatures and structures.

title: Blooming Wonders: A Celebration of Spring
description: Head to Manhattan for Blooming Wonders: A Celebration of Spring. This family event is perfect for families looking for a fun outing.

title: The Epic Circus at Epic Waters
description: Witness the legendary Luis Hernandez, a master with 36 years of elite artistry, as he conquers the soaring aerial trapeze and the Gravity Wheel. The adrenaline doesn’t stop there—watch father-son duo Jorgen and Logan Dominguez push physics to the limit with their high-speed motorcycle pursuit inside the legendary Sonic Sphere. It’s a blur of steel and skill you can’t miss! The best part? Your entrance fee into Epic Waters includes admission to the circus!

title: Dallas Arboretum Presents: Dallas Blooms
description: The largest floral festival in the Southwest returns! Combined with over 500,000 blooming bulbs including tulips, hyacinths, and daffodils, this beloved exhibit is a must-see for any Dallasite.
price: Adults ($21.95); Children ($17.95); Children 2 and under (Free); Members (Free)

title: Jurassic Elm
description: Ccome face-to-face with FOUR larger-than-life animatronic dinosaurs that move just like the real thing. It’s the perfect outdoor adventure for dino lovers of all ages!Bring the whole family to snap epic photos, see incredible moving dinosaurs up close, and learn fun and fascinating dinosaur facts. Whether you've got a future paleontologist at home or just love a good prehistoric adventure, Jurassic Elm is a can’t-miss experience.

title: Java House Grand Prix of Arlington
description: The 2.73-mile INDYCAR Grand Prix of Arlington track layout will weave through Arlington’s core sports and entertainment district and feature two iconic sporting venues recognized by fans around the world: AT&T Stadium, home of the National Football League’s Dallas Cowboys, and Globe Life Field, home of Major League Baseball’s Texas Rangers.

title: Dallas Symphony Orchestra Presents: Danny Elfman’s Music from the Films of Tim Burton
description: Audiences will experience the whimsical and dramatic scores from Burton’s most beloved films performed live by the orchestra. As the music unfolds, original sketches, drawings, and storyboards from Burton’s creative archives are projected above the stage, immersing the audience in the imaginative worlds that have defined their decades-long partnership. The coolest part? Danny Elfman will be in attendance as a special guest!

title: Train-A-Palooza Model Train Show
description: Train enthusiasts won't want to miss this! There will be several working layouts of different gauges, a Choo Choo Train Express giving rides in the parking lot, train toys, and more.

title: Spring Break at Meow Wolf Grapevine
description: Spring Break just got extra curious! Join the Brain Bean Buddies for an all-ages adventure packed with playful learning and hands-on fun. Explore the exhibition with a photo scavenger hunt, crack a plant-themed crossword, and get creative in a paper-making workshop where you’ll turn recycled materials into brand-new paper. Complete activities to earn prizes and discover how creativity, nature, and imagination grow better together.

title: Dragon Trails at The Heard
description: Embark on an enchanting journey through the mystical world of Dragons. This captivating exhibit invites visitors to explore the fascinating connections between mythical dragons and real-life creatures that have roamed our Earth throughout history. Kids will be captivated by these life-like fairy tale creatures—and just wait until they move and roar!
city: Heard Natural Science Museum and Wildlife Sanctuary
locationName: Heard Natural Science Museum and Wildlife Sanctuary
price: Adults ($16); Children 3-12 ($12); Children ages 2 & under (Free); Members (Free)

title: The FUN BUS Comes to Lakewood Brewing!
description: Celebrate St. Patrick’s Day at Lakewood Brewing Company with a day that keeps the whole family smiling! Fun Bus Dallas County is rolling up with their mobile playground. Little ones can jump, climb, slide, and explore in a safe, high-energy space led by trained coaches who keep everything running smoothly. While they’re busy having a blast, parents can relax with a locally crafted Lakewood brew and enjoy the festive atmosphere. There will be St. Patrick’s Day-themed games, music, and open play sessions happening throughout the day.

title: Coppell Arts Center Presents: Wizard of Oz on Ice
description: Instead of ruby slippers, Dorothy will be wearing sparkly ice skates for an adaptation of the classic tale as you've never seen before. Watch as world-renowned professional skating champions combine breathtaking performances with interactive elements for audiences of all ages. From Dorothy’s iconic journey down the Yellow Brick Road to the magical encounters with the Scarecrow, Tin Man, and Cowardly Lion, every moment is filled with mesmerizing choreography and stunning visuals.

title: 3-Hour Wetland Canoe Trails
description: Experience the amazing diversity in wetland vegetation and wildlife while learning about the history and basics of wetland ecology. After a safety and canoeing introduction, spend about 40 minutes on a guided trail around the Heard wetlands, followed by free paddle time to explore on your own. No experience is necessary for any of the paddle programs. All participants will be given an introduction to canoeing that includes basic strokes, boat safety, and flat water maneuvers. Minimum age: 7 years. All canoe & safety equipment provided.
city: Heard Natural Science Museum & Wildlife Sanctuary
locationName: Heard Natural Science Museum & Wildlife Sanctuary
price: Member Rate: $32 per person; Non-Member Rate: $42 per person

title: Fort Worth Museum of Science and History Presents: The Questioneers: Read. Question. Think. PLAY!
description: Put on your thinking caps as the imaginative world of the Questioneers comes alive in this hands-on exhibit! Fly with Rosie in her cheese-copter, question how the world works while sitting in Ada's thinking chair, build a bridge to save the class with Iggy, lead a movement with Sofia while playing a game in Sofia's Citizen Park, and tell a story through drawings like Aaron!
city: Fort Worth Museum of  Science and History
locationName: Fort Worth Museum of  Science and History

title: Butterflies in the Garden at the Fort Worth Botanic Garden
description: Butterfly season has arrived, and the largest exhibit of live, exotic butterflies is in the garden. Have the chance to see a diverse collection of butterflies from various parts of the world, including Central, North, and South America, Africa, and Asia. The Blue Morpho (Morpho peleides), known for its striking beauty, is one of the most popular butterflies on display. Additionally, local favorites will also be showcased. If you want to visit the rest of the garden, you'll need to pay general admission as well.
price: Adults ($12); Kids ages 3-15 ($8); Kids ages 2 & under (Free); This is for the exhibit only.

title: Perot Museum of Science and Nature Presents: Soccer: More Than a Game
description: The World Cup will host games in DFW this year, and the Perot is celebrating! Experience the excitement of the world’s most beloved sport in a fast-paced, kid-friendly exhibition that reveals how physics, biology, and technology shape every aspect of the game, on and off the field.
city: Perot Museum of Nature and Science
locationName: Perot Museum of Nature and Science
price: Adults after admission ($12); Children after admission ($10); Members receive discounted prices

title: FL!P Circus at Riders Field
description: Brought to you by the iconic Circus Vasquez, this entirely new show is filled with dazzling feats, including aerialists, acrobats, jugglers, dancers, and a crowd-favorite clown, all during an intimate big-top experience where every seat is close to the ring. And don't forget about the FL!P Circus Band, which threads the lineup into one story, playing in real time so drum hits meet flips and brass swells crown the poses!

title: Spring Break in the Park
description: Klyde Warren Park is a hub of fun for spring break in Dallas! Enjoy all sorts of kid-friendly activities like the Perot Museum Tech Truck, Art on the Go with SMU Meadows Museum, ballet classes, Movies in the Park on March 21 (Zootopia 2), star gazing with Cosmic Tours, and so much more! There will also be live music, and of course, food trucks! March 13.

title: Dallas Holocaust and Human Rights Museum Presents: The Walt Disney Studios and World War II
description: Step behind the magic and into history with The Walt Disney Studios and World War II. Discover how Disney transformed its studio into a wartime operation producing original artwork, as well as training and public-service films, and how artists, employees, and Walt Disney himself contributed to the war effort. With more than 500 rare artifacts, film clips, and stories of innovation and sacrifice, this perfect for all ages exhibit explores how one of America’s most beloved entertainment companies helped achieve the Allied victory.

title: Tea Around Town Dallas
description: Experience the vibrant energy of Tea Around Town in Dallas. Our iconic pink bus is adorned with beautiful blooms, creating a joyful backdrop as you discover the heart of Texas. Sip on fine tea, indulge in delectable treats, and sing along with our entertainers as you pass by landmarks like Reunion Tower, Dealey Plaza, and the Dallas Arts District!

title: Balloon Museum Dallas Presents: “Let’s Fly – Art Has No Limits”
description: A multi-sensory exhibit that combines air, art, and creativity takes you on a journey like never before! Large-scale artworks from 18 world-renowned artists range from an interactive butterfly to suspended spheres set in motion by audience interaction in this first-time Dallas pop-up art experience. Unlike traditional museums, visitors will be using sight, touch, and sound; an invitation to explore art in a whole new way.

title: Bingo in the Hall
description: Don't feel like cooking for your crew? Head to Harvest Hall to grab a bite and play a few rounds of bingo with the kids! Choose between pizza, Mediterranean food, Mexican street food, coastal cuisine, and more.

description: Witness the legendary Luis Hernandez, a master with 36 years of elite artistry, as he conquers the soaring aerial trapeze and the Gravity Wheel. It’s a blur of steel and skill you can’t miss! The best part? Your entrance fee into Epic Waters includes admission to the circus!

There will also be live music, and of course, food trucks! March 13.

title: Dallas Holocaust and Human Rights Museum Presents: The Walt Disney Studios and World War II
description: Step behind the magic and into history with The Walt Disney Studios and World War II. Discover how Disney transformed its studio into a wartime operation producing original artwork, as well as training and public-service films, and how artists, employees, and Walt Disney himself contributed to the war effort. With more than 500 rare artifacts, film clips, and stories of innovation and sacrifice, this great for families exhibit explores how one of America’s most beloved entertainment companies helped achieve the Allied victory.

title: Nash Farm Presents: Spring Break on the Farm
description: It's a whole week of farm fun for spring break! Whether you’re churning butter, making soap, or planting a garden, it's the perfect way to learn and play in Grapevine’s rich heritage. You can look forward to making sheep toys out of wool, playing 19th-century games, an afternoon picnic, and much more.

title: Tea Around Town Dallas
description: Experience the vibrant energy of Tea Around Town in Dallas. Our iconic pink bus is adorned with beautiful blooms, creating a joyful backdrop as you discover the heart of Texas. Sip on fine tea, indulge in delectable treats, and sing along with our entertainers as you pass by landmarks like Reunion Tower, Dealey Plaza, and the Dallas Arts District!

title: Balloon Museum Dallas Presents: “Let’s Fly – Art Has No Limits”
description: A multi-sensory exhibit that combines air, art, and creativity takes you on a journey like never before! Large-scale artworks from 18 world-renowned artists range from an interactive butterfly to suspended spheres set in motion by audience interaction in this first-time Dallas pop-up art experience. Unlike traditional museums, visitors will be using sight, touch, and sound; an invitation to explore art in a whole new way.

title: The Epic Circus at Epic Waters
description: Witness the legendary Luis Hernandez, a master with 36 years of elite artistry, as he conquers the soaring aerial trapeze and the Gravity Wheel. The adrenaline doesn’t stop there—watch father-son duo Jorgen and Logan Dominguez push physics to the limit with their high-speed motorcycle pursuit inside the legendary Sonic Sphere. It’s a blur of steel and skill you can’t miss! The best part? Your entrance fee into Epic Waters includes admission to the circus!

title: Dallas Arboretum Presents: Dallas Blooms
description: The largest floral festival in the Southwest returns! Combined with over 500,000 blooming bulbs including tulips, hyacinths, and daffodils, this beloved exhibit is a must-see for any Dallasite.
price: Adults ($21.95); Children ($17.95); Children 2 and under (Free); Members (Free)

title: Jurassic Elm
description: Ccome face-to-face with FOUR larger-than-life animatronic dinosaurs that move just like the real thing. It’s the perfect outdoor adventure for dino lovers of all ages!Bring the whole family to snap epic photos, see incredible moving dinosaurs up close, and learn fun and fascinating dinosaur facts. Whether you've got a future paleontologist at home or just love a good prehistoric adventure, Jurassic Elm is a can’t-miss experience.

title: Spring Break at Meow Wolf Grapevine
description: Spring Break just got extra curious! Join the Brain Bean Buddies for an all-ages adventure packed with playful learning and hands-on fun. Explore the exhibition with a photo scavenger hunt, crack a plant-themed crossword, and get creative in a paper-making workshop where you’ll turn recycled materials into brand-new paper. Complete activities to earn prizes and discover how creativity, nature, and imagination grow better together.

title: Dragon Trails at The Heard
description: Embark on an enchanting journey through the mystical world of Dragons. This captivating exhibit invites visitors to explore the fascinating connections between mythical dragons and real-life creatures that have roamed our Earth throughout history. Kids will be captivated by these life-like fairy tale creatures—and just wait until they move and roar!
city: Heard Natural Science Museum and Wildlife Sanctuary
locationName: Heard Natural Science Museum and Wildlife Sanctuary
price: Adults ($16); Children 3-12 ($12); Children ages 2 & under (Free); Members (Free)

title: Spring Break Art Extravaganza at the Kimbell
description: Get inspired during spring break! Enjoy free activities that include short family films, story time, printmaking, making suncatchers, and dress-up self-portraits. Melt ice cream will be available for purchase!

title: Spring Break at the Carter
description: Hanging around DFW for spring break? The Amon Carter Museum invites you to drop in for several free and fun events just for families. Think gallery games, art-making activities, creativity challenges, and more. Advance registration is suggested.
city: Amon Carter Museum of American Art
locationName: Amon Carter Museum of American Art

title: Fort Worth Museum of Science and History Presents: The Questioneers: Read. Question. Think. PLAY!
description: Put on your thinking caps as the imaginative world of the Questioneers comes alive in this hands-on exhibit! Fly with Rosie in her cheese-copter, question how the world works while sitting in Ada's thinking chair, build a bridge to save the class with Iggy, lead a movement with Sofia while playing a game in Sofia's Citizen Park, and tell a story through drawings like Aaron!
city: Fort Worth Museum of  Science and History
locationName: Fort Worth Museum of  Science and History

title: Butterflies in the Garden at the Fort Worth Botanic Garden
description: Butterfly season has arrived, and the largest exhibit of live, exotic butterflies is in the garden. Have the chance to see a diverse collection of butterflies from various parts of the world, including Central, North, and South America, Africa, and Asia. The Blue Morpho (Morpho peleides), known for its striking beauty, is one of the most popular butterflies on display. Additionally, local favorites will also be showcased. If you want to visit the rest of the garden, you'll need to pay general admission as well.
price: Adults ($12); Kids ages 3-15 ($8); Kids ages 2 & under (Free); This is for the exhibit only.

title: Perot Museum of Science and Nature Presents: Soccer: More Than a Game
description: The World Cup will host games in DFW this year, and the Perot is celebrating! Experience the excitement of the world’s most beloved sport in a fast-paced, perfect for all ages exhibition that reveals how physics, biology, and technology shape every aspect of the game, on and off the field.
city: Perot Museum of Nature and Science
locationName: Perot Museum of Nature and Science
price: Adults after admission ($12); Children after admission ($10); Members receive discounted prices

description: Butterfly season has arrived, and the largest exhibit of live, exotic butterflies is in the garden. Have the chance to see a diverse collection of butterflies from various parts of the world, including Central, North, and South America, Africa, and Asia. The Blue Morpho (Morpho peleides), known for its striking beauty, is one of the most popular butterflies on display. Additionally, local favorites will also be showcased. If you want to visit the rest of the garden, you'll need to pay general admission as well.
price: Adults ($12); Kids ages 3-15 ($8); Kids ages 2 & under (Free); This is for the exhibit only.

title: Perot Museum of Science and Nature Presents: Soccer: More Than a Game
description: The World Cup will host games in DFW this year, and the Perot is celebrating! Experience the excitement of the world’s most beloved sport in a fast-paced, perfect for all ages exhibition that reveals how physics, biology, and technology shape every aspect of the game, on and off the field.
city: Perot Museum of Nature and Science
locationName: Perot Museum of Nature and Science
price: Adults after admission ($12); Children after admission ($10); Members receive discounted prices

title: FL!P Circus at Riders Field
description: Brought to you by the iconic Circus Vasquez, this entirely new show is filled with dazzling feats, including aerialists, acrobats, jugglers, dancers, and a crowd-favorite clown, all during an intimate big-top experience where every seat is close to the ring. And don't forget about the FL!P Circus Band, which threads the lineup into one story, playing in real time so drum hits meet flips and brass swells crown the poses!

title: Spring Break in the Park
description: Klyde Warren Park is a hub of fun for spring break in Dallas! Enjoy all sorts of kid-friendly activities like the Perot Museum Tech Truck, Art on the Go with SMU Meadows Museum, ballet classes, Movies in the Park on March 21 (Zootopia 2), star gazing with Cosmic Tours, and so much more! There will also be live music, and of course, food trucks! March 13.

title: Dallas Holocaust and Human Rights Museum Presents: The Walt Disney Studios and World War II
description: Step behind the magic and into history with The Walt Disney Studios and World War II. Discover how Disney transformed its studio into a wartime operation producing original artwork, as well as training and public-service films, and how artists, employees, and Walt Disney himself contributed to the war effort. With more than 500 rare artifacts, film clips, and stories of innovation and sacrifice, this kid-friendly exhibit explores how one of America’s most beloved entertainment companies helped achieve the Allied victory.

title: Nash Farm Presents: Spring Break on the Farm
description: It's a whole week of farm fun for spring break! Whether you’re churning butter, making soap, or planting a garden, it's the perfect way to learn and play in Grapevine’s rich heritage. Planned activities feature making sheep toys out of wool, playing 19th-century games, an afternoon picnic, and much more.

title: Spring Break Family Fun at the Dallas Art Museum
description: Explore your artistic side during spring break at the museum! There will be special activities each day, including pop-up art spots, Little Lights Glow Play, story time, sensory play, and more.

title: Toddler Storytime at Bookmarks in NorthPark
description: Join in on the fun during storytime at Bookmarks! Get ready to sing, jump, listen, and make friends.

title: Storytime at the Interurban Railway Museum
description: A fantastic option for families: the museum for storytime with Motorman Harold! Stay for a tour of Historic Car 360.

title: Kid's Night Out at Pipe & Palette
description: Drop your kids off to paint, watch a movie, and eat pizza while you enjoy alone time. Kids can paint, watch a movie, and eat pizza while parents get alone time. Check the website for movie info. Pre-booking is required for all attendees.

title: Tea Around Town Dallas
description: Experience the vibrant energy of Tea Around Town in Dallas. Our iconic pink bus is adorned with beautiful blooms, creating a joyful backdrop as you discover the heart of Texas. Sip on fine tea, indulge in delectable treats, and sing along with our entertainers as you pass by landmarks like Reunion Tower, Dealey Plaza, and the Dallas Arts District!

title: Balloon Museum Dallas Presents: “Let’s Fly – Art Has No Limits”
description: A multi-sensory exhibit that combines air, art, and creativity takes you on a journey like never before! Large-scale artworks from 18 world-renowned artists range from an interactive butterfly to suspended spheres set in motion by audience interaction in this first-time Dallas pop-up art experience. Unlike traditional museums, visitors will be using sight, touch, and sound; an invitation to explore art in a whole new way.

title: Movie Night in the District
description: Head to the PGA District for free Friday movie nights! The district restaurants will be open for you to grab a bite, and roaming concession carts will be around during the movie. Blankets are encouraged, and pets are welcome if kept on a leash.

title: The Epic Circus at Epic Waters
description: Witness the legendary Luis Hernandez, a master with 36 years of elite artistry, as he conquers the soaring aerial trapeze and the Gravity Wheel. The adrenaline doesn’t stop there—watch father-son duo Jorgen and Logan Dominguez push physics to the limit with their high-speed motorcycle pursuit inside the legendary Sonic Sphere. It’s a blur of steel and skill you can’t miss! The best part? Your entrance fee into Epic Waters includes admission to the circus!

title: Dallas Arboretum Presents: Dallas Blooms
description: The largest floral festival in the Southwest returns! Combined with over 500,000 blooming bulbs including tulips, hyacinths, and daffodils, this beloved exhibit is a must-see for any Dallasite.
price: Adults ($21.95); Children ($17.95); Children 2 and under (Free); Members (Free)

title: Jurassic Elm
description: Ccome face-to-face with FOUR larger-than-life animatronic dinosaurs that move just like the real thing. It’s the perfect outdoor adventure for dino lovers of all ages!Bring the whole family to snap epic photos, see incredible moving dinosaurs up close, and learn fun and fascinating dinosaur facts. Whether you've got a future paleontologist at home or just love a good prehistoric adventure, Jurassic Elm is a can’t-miss experience.

title: Spring Break at Meow Wolf Grapevine
description: Spring Break just got extra curious! Join the Brain Bean Buddies for an all-ages adventure packed with playful learning and hands-on fun. Explore the exhibition with a photo scavenger hunt, crack a plant-themed crossword, and get creative in a paper-making workshop where you’ll turn recycled materials into brand-new paper. Complete activities to earn prizes and discover how creativity, nature, and imagination grow better together.

title: Dragon Trails at The Heard
description: Embark on an enchanting journey through the mystical world of Dragons. This captivating exhibit invites visitors to explore the fascinating connections between mythical dragons and real-life creatures that have roamed our Earth throughout history. Kids will be captivated by these life-like fairy tale creatures—and just wait until they move and roar!
city: Heard Natural Science Museum and Wildlife Sanctuary
locationName: Heard Natural Science Museum and Wildlife Sanctuary
price: Adults ($16); Children 3-12 ($12); Children ages 2 & under (Free); Members (Free)

title: Aqua Egg Hunt
description: A warm pool? Check! Floating eggs? Check! This Easter egg hunt is one of the coolest in all of DFW. Get a photo with the Easter Bunny, hit up the temporary tattoo station, snag a goody bag, and enjoy the pool for the rest of the day. Tickets are set up by age and time slot.

title: Flower Mound Holi Festival of Colors
description: Happy Holi! This incredible family event celebrates the vibrant tradition of India's Festival of Colors. Enjoy color throwing, dancing, food, a kids' parade, games, and even a color run!

title: Texas Forever Fest 2026
description: There’s no friendlier place than the Lone Star state, and this festival is rolling out the welcome mat for the whole community. Enjoy live music, entertainment, activities, a kids' zone, food, drinks, and vendors all influenced by Texas’ diverse culture and spirit.

title: Fort Worth Museum of Science and History Presents: The Questioneers: Read. Question. Think. PLAY!
description: Put on your thinking caps as the imaginative world of the Questioneers comes alive in this hands-on exhibit! Fly with Rosie in her cheese-copter, question how the world works while sitting in Ada's thinking chair, build a bridge to save the class with Iggy, lead a movement with Sofia while playing a game in Sofia's Citizen Park, and tell a story through drawings like Aaron!

Fly with Rosie in her cheese-copter, question how the world works while sitting in Ada's thinking chair, build a bridge to save the class with Iggy, lead a movement with Sofia while playing a game in Sofia's Citizen Park, and tell a story through drawings like Aaron!
city: Fort Worth Museum of  Science and History
locationName: Fort Worth Museum of  Science and History

title: Butterflies in the Garden at the Fort Worth Botanic Garden
description: Butterfly season has arrived, and the largest exhibit of live, exotic butterflies is in the garden. Have the chance to see a diverse collection of butterflies from various parts of the world, including Central, North, and South America, Africa, and Asia. The Blue Morpho (Morpho peleides), known for its striking beauty, is one of the most popular butterflies on display. Additionally, local favorites will also be showcased. If you want to visit the rest of the garden, you'll need to pay general admission as well.
price: Adults ($12); Kids ages 3-15 ($8); Kids ages 2 & under (Free); This is for the exhibit only.

title: Perot Museum of Science and Nature Presents: Soccer: More Than a Game
description: The World Cup will host games in DFW this year, and the Perot is celebrating! Experience the excitement of the world’s most beloved sport in a fast-paced, great for families exhibition that reveals how physics, biology, and technology shape every aspect of the game, on and off the field.
city: Perot Museum of Nature and Science
locationName: Perot Museum of Nature and Science
price: Adults after admission ($12); Children after admission ($10); Members receive discounted prices

title: FL!P Circus at Riders Field
description: Brought to you by the iconic Circus Vasquez, this entirely new show is filled with dazzling feats, including aerialists, acrobats, jugglers, dancers, and a crowd-favorite clown, all during an intimate big-top experience where every seat is close to the ring. And don't forget about the FL!P Circus Band, which threads the lineup into one story, playing in real time so drum hits meet flips and brass swells crown the poses!

title: Dallas Holocaust and Human Rights Museum Presents: The Walt Disney Studios and World War II
description: Step behind the magic and into history with The Walt Disney Studios and World War II. Discover how Disney transformed its studio into a wartime operation producing original artwork, as well as training and public-service films, and how artists, employees, and Walt Disney himself contributed to the war effort. With more than 500 rare artifacts, film clips, and stories of innovation and sacrifice, this welcoming to families exhibit explores how one of America’s most beloved entertainment companies helped achieve the Allied victory.

title: Tea Around Town Dallas
description: Experience the vibrant energy of Tea Around Town in Dallas. Our iconic pink bus is adorned with beautiful blooms, creating a joyful backdrop as you discover the heart of Texas. Sip on fine tea, indulge in delectable treats, and sing along with our entertainers as you pass by landmarks like Reunion Tower, Dealey Plaza, and the Dallas Arts District!

title: Balloon Museum Dallas Presents: “Let’s Fly – Art Has No Limits”
description: A multi-sensory exhibit that combines air, art, and creativity takes you on a journey like never before! Large-scale artworks from 18 world-renowned artists range from an interactive butterfly to suspended spheres set in motion by audience interaction in this first-time Dallas pop-up art experience. Unlike traditional museums, visitors will be using sight, touch, and sound; an invitation to explore art in a whole new way.

title: Dallas Arboretum Presents: Dallas Blooms
description: The largest floral festival in the Southwest returns! Combined with over 500,000 blooming bulbs including tulips, hyacinths, and daffodils, this beloved exhibit is a must-see for any Dallasite.
price: Adults ($21.95); Children ($17.95); Children 2 and under (Free); Members (Free)

title: Dragon Trails at The Heard
description: Embark on an enchanting journey through the mystical world of Dragons. This captivating exhibit invites visitors to explore the fascinating connections between mythical dragons and real-life creatures that have roamed our Earth throughout history. Kids will be captivated by these life-like fairy tale creatures—and just wait until they move and roar!
city: Heard Natural Science Museum and Wildlife Sanctuary
locationName: Heard Natural Science Museum and Wildlife Sanctuary
price: Adults ($16); Children 3-12 ($12); Children ages 2 & under (Free); Members (Free)

title: Movie Night at The Star: Lilo and Stitch
description: On Wednesday, March 18, meet at The Star in Frisco for a Movie Night screening of Lilo and Stitch, presented by Monument Realty, on Tostitos Championship Plaza. The plaza opens.

title: Challenge Air Fly Day
description: Dallas-based nonprofit Challenge Air Fly Day provides a once-in-a-lifetime experience for those special needs ages of 7–21 of co-piloting an aircraft over Mesquite.
locationName: Commemorative Air Force at Dallas Executive Airport

title: Mansfield St. Paddy’s Pickle Parade & Palooza
description: Get a double dose of green at the St. Paddy’s Pickle Parade & Palooza, City of Mansfield's spring festival in celebration of St. Patrick’s Day and Mansfield’s heritage crop.

title: Chicago Theatre Week: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory
description: Based on the beloved novel by Roald Dahl, enjoy this delightfully scrumptious musical adaptation that brings to life all of your favourite characters, songs, and confections. A gobstoppingly good time for the whole family! Grab your golden ticket and join Charlie Bucket on this delectable adventure into Willy Wonka’s world famous chocolate factory!

title: Sap's Rising
description: Families can enjoy a walk through the sugar maple forest and learn how maple syrup is made. This activity repeats every weekend during the first three weekend of March. Registration is required.

title: Robin Hood at the Mudlark Theatre
description: Who is under the hood? In a tavern at the edge of Sherwood Forest, rebels convene to find the infamous outlaw Robin Hood after Prince John has ascended the throne. Everyone knows something about Robin Hood, but no one knows exactly who he is. As the threat from the lords of the land grows, can the rebels find their hero and stand up against the rising tyranny?

title: St. Patrick's Festival at the Irish American Heritage Center
description: Celebrate all things Irish at the Irish American Heritage Center following the downtown St. Patrick's Day parade. Highlights include contemporary Irish music, Irish dance, arts and crafts, vendors, exhibits, Irish language lessons, genealogy workshops, and a book sale.
price: $30 Adult (advanced purchase), $35 Adult (at the door), FREE children under 12

title: Sugar Bush Fair
description: Spring Valley Nature Center's annual celebration of maple sugar is sweet. See demonstrations of how maple syrup and enjoy historic reenactments.
city: Vera Meineke Nature Center at Spring Valley
locationName: Vera Meineke Nature Center at Spring Valley

title: Maple Syrup Festival at River Trail Nature Center
description: This festival includes tree tapping, maple syrup samples, games, and crafts. You will even get to learn a bit about the history of maple syrup.

title: South Side Irish Parade
description: The South Side Irish Parade will step off at noon from 103rd & Western and proceed south down Western Avenue to 115th Street. The Parade is one of the largest community-based St. Patrick's Day Parades outside of Ireland.

title: Northwest Side Irish Parade
description: The 23rd Annual Northwest Side Irish Parade will kick off at noon at Onahan School. The parade will end around 1:30 pm at Harlem Ave. The official after-party will begin at 1 pm at Zia's Social.

title: Peggy Notebaert Nature Museum Family Story Time Yoga
description: Join Buddha Belly Kids Yoga for a welcoming to families yoga class at the Peggy Notebaert Nature Museum. The class, designed for 2- to 8-year-old kids and their grownups, focuses on moving, breathing, and connecting. The yoga will follow the narrative of the story.

title: Vivid Creatures at the Morton Arboretum
description: Vivid Creatures, a new outdoor art exhibition at The Morton Arboretum, is an invitation to explore connections with the natural world through giant, colorful, playful animal sculptures placed outdoors amid towering trees and sweeping green vistas. Each artwork tells a story about the relationships between creatures and the ecosystems where they live.

title: I AM WILD: A Charley Harper Exhibit
description: I AM WILD: A Charley Harper Exhibit explores the flora and fauna of six different habitats, including the Desert, Ocean, Rainforest, Sky, Woods and City. The exhibit invites children and families to discover the diversity and interdependency of life in the natural world, fostering an appreciation for its creatures. Exhibit Features Interactive Habitat Poster Kiosks – Multi-sensory interpretations of Harper’s iconic posters representing each of the featured habitats.

title: Sensory-Friendly Time at Build-A-Bear Workshop
description: Bear builders help children build a bear in a way that is comfortable for them with a sensory friendly first hour of business every Tuesday morning. description: Meet Roxanne, Petunia, and Babe…three Sister Piggies who set out to build their own houses. title: Family Egg Hunt Adventure at Lincoln Marsh
description: Find eggs using a series of clues Easter egg hunts in Lincoln Marsh. It’s a perfect way to combine outdoor exploration with a dash of springtime magic. Families will search for eggs along the trails of Lincoln Marsh.

title: Easter Egg Hunt at the Wildflower Farm
description: Looking for a fun and stress-free Easter egg hunt? Unlike traditional egg hunts that can feel like a mad dash, The Wildflower Farm's egg hunt is relaxed and enjoyable for everyone. Each child with a child’s ticket is guaranteed 12 eggs and a basket that they can decorate before starting their hunt. Eggs are hidden across 12 designated egg stations—no competition, just fun! Arrive anytime during your selected time slot to enjoy the experience at your own pace. Fresh pretzels will be available for purchase, and children can have their faces painted.

title: Palos Heights Easter Egg Hunt
description: Palos Heights Christian Reformed Church invites families to participate in its egg hunt and learn more about the church. A light breakfast will be served.

title: Bunny Hop at Hale Park
description: Hop over to Hale Park to enjoy music, dancing, crafts, an egg hunt, face painting, and a raffle. Stick around to get your picture taken with the Easter bunny.

title: Candy Land Carnival
description: Step right up into world of color, candy, and carnival fun! Candy Land comes to life at this whimsical event, where kids can: 🍭 Twirl down Lollipop Lane🤹‍♂️ Play Gumdrop Games🍬 Enjoy Candy-Themed Activities📸 Meet Magical CharactersA carnival of fun awaits & it’s too sweet to miss. 🦄.

title: Vivid Creatures at the Morton Arboretum
description: Vivid Creatures, a new outdoor art exhibition at The Morton Arboretum, is an invitation to explore connections with the natural world through giant, colorful, playful animal sculptures placed outdoors amid towering trees and sweeping green vistas. Each artwork tells a story about the relationships between creatures and the ecosystems where they live.

title: I AM WILD: A Charley Harper Exhibit
description: I AM WILD: A Charley Harper Exhibit explores the flora and fauna of six different habitats, including the Desert, Ocean, Rainforest, Sky, Woods and City. The exhibit invites children and families to discover the diversity and interdependency of life in the natural world, fostering an appreciation for its creatures. Exhibit Features Interactive Habitat Poster Kiosks – Multi-sensory interpretations of Harper’s iconic posters representing each of the featured habitats.

title: Owl Moon Fireside
description: Hear the delightful story, “Owl Moon,” about a young girl who goes owling with her father in winter, and learn how to “whoo, whoo” like Great Horned Owls searching for a mate. Then we’re headed outdoors to call to the owls, so dress for the weather. The fireside concludes with hot chocolate and a look at owl pellets.

title: Chicago Theatre Week: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory
description: Based on the beloved novel by Roald Dahl, enjoy this delightfully scrumptious musical adaptation that brings to life all of your favourite characters, songs, and confections. A gobstoppingly good time for the whole family! Grab your golden ticket and join Charlie Bucket on this delectable adventure into Willy Wonka’s world famous chocolate factory!

title: Robin Hood at the Mudlark Theatre
description: Who is under the hood? In a tavern at the edge of Sherwood Forest, rebels convene to find the infamous outlaw Robin Hood after Prince John has ascended the throne. Everyone knows something about Robin Hood, but no one knows exactly who he is. As the threat from the lords of the land grows, can the rebels find their hero and stand up against the rising tyranny?

title: Ramble On: EDE Rocks!
description: The Evanston Dance Ensemble is diving into the archives. See themexplore the culture, attitude, and expansive reach of rock and roll music. Artistic Director Enid Smith and Assistant to the Director Macala Bartucci are joined by guests Kaleigh Dent, Julie Cartier, Tori Isaac, Ro Goodman, and EDE founding director Béa Rashid in this electrifying evening of dance.

title: Easter Egg Hunt at the Wildflower Farm
description: Looking for a fun and stress-free Easter egg hunt? Unlike traditional egg hunts that can feel like a mad dash, The Wildflower Farm's egg hunt is relaxed and enjoyable for everyone. Each child with a child’s ticket is guaranteed 12 eggs and a basket that they can decorate before starting their hunt. Eggs are hidden across 12 designated egg stations—no competition, just fun! Arrive anytime during your selected time slot to enjoy the experience at your own pace. Fresh pretzels will be available for purchase, and children can have their faces painted.

title: Vivid Creatures at the Morton Arboretum
description: Vivid Creatures, a new outdoor art exhibition at The Morton Arboretum, is an invitation to explore connections with the natural world through giant, colorful, playful animal sculptures placed outdoors amid towering trees and sweeping green vistas. Each artwork tells a story about the relationships between creatures and the ecosystems where they live.

title: Budding Botanist
description: On Saturdays and Sundays from 11:00am to 3:00pm, Nature Educators will provide activities for families with children of all ages at The Garfield Park Conservatory's Budding Botanists station. These programs are drop in and no preregistration is required. You can stop by and interact with the materials at the station for as long as you want to. Budding Botanists station will be posted in different areas of the Conservatory on different days. Ask where they are at the front desk! The location will be posted in the lobby each day.

title: Sensory-Friendly Time at Build-A-Bear Workshop
description: Bear builders help children build a bear in a way that is comfortable for them with a sensory friendly first hour of business every Tuesday morning. Each store will have dimmer lighting, little or no music, and a step-by-step information guide to help parents tell their children what will happen in advance. Siblings are welcome!
city: Build a Bear Workshops Chicago area locations
locationName: Build a Bear Workshops Chicago area locations

title: Bunny Hop at Welles Park
description: Join the Chicago Park District for a hopping good time at our Bunny Hop event designed especially for children under 6 years old. Get ready for a day filled with fun games, bouncing excitement in the bounce house, a creative art craft project, an egg hunt to find sweet surprises, and don't forget to strike a pose and take a picture with the Bunny himself! Bring your little ones for an unforgettable day of laughter and joy.

title: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs
description: The Queen is jealous of Snow White’s Beauty! So she runs off into the woods to live with seven crazy Dwarfs. Will the Queen find her? Will Prince Charming save the day? You won’t want to miss this classic Fairy Tale presented by CKC. There is nothing scary in our version, just fun and silliness!

title: The Three Little Pigs at Beverly Arts Center
description: Meet Roxanne, Petunia, and Babe…three Sister Piggies who set out to build their own houses. Roxanne (the Rock’n Roll Pig) builds hers out of Sticks, Petunia (The Pretty Pig) builds hers out of Straw, and Babe (the sensible, smart Pig) builds hers out of bricks! Which house will the Big Bad Wolf Huff and Puff and blow down? Don’t miss CKC’s most popular show!

title: Sensory Egg Hunt at Mount Greenwood Park
description: Perfect for a family day out — Mount Greenwood Park for a special egg hunt for kids with sensory needs. Registration is required.

title: Donation Days for Illinois Residents at the Notebaert Nature Museum
description: Admission is free (suggested donation) for all Illinois residents every Thursday the museum is open. The Peggy Notebaert Museum has so much to offer families. Explore the Riverworks display, unlock the Mysteries of the Marsh, stroll through the Wilderness Walk, and have fun in the Hands-On Exhibit. The Butterfly Haven is a can't miss experience. Check with the museum for special hours on holidays.

title: C2E2: Chicago Comic and Entertainment Expo at McCormick Place
description: Get geeky at C2E2, Chicago's annual comic and pop culture convention at McCormick Place. Kids might be the most interested in the Family Play Zone, where they can play interactive games, learn to draw their favorite characters, and have fun in the free play zone. Favorite characterswill be there to meet and greet with kids.

title: West Chicago Glow in the Dark Easter Egg Hunt
description: Hop over to Reed-Keppler Park to search for glow-in-the-dark eggs that can be turned in for prizes. The Easter Bunny will also be hopping around for pictures.
price: $10 per West Chicago resident; $12 per non-resident

title: Warrenville Flashlight Egg Hunt
description: Hunt for eggs after dark at Bower Elementary School in Warrenville. The Easter Bunny will hide special eggs that can be traded for candy at the end of the hunt.
